event: An angel holding a lamp amid flowers
symbols: Angel with wings — a heavenly messenger, a servant of God
Lamp or light held by the angel — divine illumination, guidance, and watchfulness
Flowers and vegetation — the created world tended by angelic guardianship
description: A slender angel with large, warm-toned feathered wings stands amid flowering bushes. The angel has flowing reddish-brown hair and wears a white robe with a purple-blue overlay. The figure holds something close to the chest — possibly a lamp or vessel. The rendering is delicate and transparent, in watercolor tones of amber, blue, green, and cream. The angel's expression is serene and watchful, gazing to the side. Flowers bloom around the lower portion of the composition.
theological_description: Angels as light-bearers and guardians appear throughout Scripture. The psalmist declares: 'He will command his angels concerning you to guard you in all your ways' (PSA.91.11). The lamp carried by this angel evokes the parable of the wise virgins who kept their lamps burning (MAT.25.1-13) and the broader biblical theme of light as divine presence: 'Your word is a lamp to my feet and a light to my path' (PSA.119.105). The angel among flowers suggests the guardian of creation — Hebrews describes angels as 'ministering spirits sent out to serve for the sake of those who are to inherit salvation' (HEB.1.14). The gentle, watchful posture embodies the protective ministry of angels that Jesus affirmed when He said of children: 'Their angels in heaven always see the face of my Father who is in heaven' (MAT.18.10).

scripture_refs.reason: He will command His angels concerning you to guard you — the guardian angel's protective role
Ministering spirits sent to serve those who inherit salvation — the angel's serving role
Their angels in heaven always see the Father's face — guardian angels watching over the faithful
God's word as a lamp — the light the angel carries as symbol of divine guidance
